the single player content in the game may also be instructive, if you haven't played that yet. playing master bot might also be helpful depending on your skill level but only to a point - once you can beat it somewhat reliably i'm not sure there's much value in continuing to play against it, and it might be better to jump into pvp matches after that point and then trying to analyze why you lost afterwards. personally i found streams by top players (like msven and apooche) to be the most helpful in terms of learning, but there aren't many of those these days

--- Thread (10 messages, 2019-11-19) ---
[2019-11-19 07:07] jamesa7171: both (and they kind of reinforce each other). community morale is a bit low right now, the devs are still working on the game but we're kind of in the dark about what exactly is happening and when. new campaign content is very long overdue and there was supposed to be a huge marketing push like...last year? which apparently still hasn't happened yet. so the game is kind of in limbo right now, and people have held optimism and hope for a very long period of time but eventually it gets exhausting and people become jaded until tangible progress actually gets demonstrated
[2019-11-19 07:07] jamesa7171: having said that the game itself is still solid and still worthy of playing
[2019-11-19 07:09] axellspite: Thats unfortunate. From watching gameplay footage on youtube the game feels like chess but taken in a different direction. Which is what im loving so far.
[2019-11-19 07:09] axellspite: but without a player base to play against, idk how much ill stay on this game.
[2019-11-19 07:13] jamesa7171: yeah, no player base is definitely a problem as it's a self-perpetuating cycle: people don't play, so there's nobody to play against, which causes people not to play, etc. but this does mean that if people do start playing again for whatever reason (let's say some updates finally happen) that it could snowball the other way as the people who stopped playing because others weren't playing would also come back
[2019-11-19 07:13] jamesa7171: for the time being you'll still be able to find people to play against, it just might be awkward matchmaking wise (like you might get matched up against really good players because there's nobody of your skill level online, which could suck)
[2019-11-19 07:15] jamesa7171: i think there are some other newer players who post here on discord once in a while, could be mutually beneficial to get in touch with them and play some friendly games that way
[2019-11-19 07:15] axellspite: yea i can see how thats a problem. If the matchmaking system is going to be awkward, i dont think id play it much. I dont usually like to partake in my butt getting a big ol' can of whoop.
[2019-11-19 07:16] axellspite: maybe ill just stick to some bots to learn the game and when the player base comes back ill try some pvp. 

Thank you James for the help, its been nice talking to you. I gotta hit the hay.
[2019-11-19 07:16] jamesa7171: you too 👍 hope you enjoy the game still in some form
